annotations.label: Classical orthogonal polynomials

anchor.snippet: the classical orthogonal polynomials are the most widely used orthogonal polynomials

annotations.note: Mathlib has scattered classical families (Chebyshev T/U, probabilist Hermite, shifted Legendre) but no unifying notion of 'classical orthogonal polynomial' nor a Jacobi/Laguerre/Gegenbauer family.

annotations.label: Characterization by differential equation
annotations.note: No general statement that classical orthogonal polynomials are exactly those satisfying a Sturm–Liouville-type ODE exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Orthogonal polynomials with respect to a weight
annotations.note: Mathlib has no general definition of an orthogonal polynomial system with respect to a weight measure on an interval.

annotations.label: Three families of weights

anchor.snippet: The classical orthogonal polynomials correspond to the following three families of weights

annotations.note: The classification of weights (Jacobi, Laguerre, Hermite) is not codified anywhere in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Jacobi polynomials formula

anchor.snippet: the Jacobi polynomials are given by the formula

annotations.note: Jacobi polynomials are not defined in Mathlib (only the unrelated Jacobi symbol/theta/sum decls).

annotations.label: Jacobi polynomial normalisation
annotations.note: No Jacobi polynomials in Mathlib so the normalisation P_n^{(α,β)}(1) = (n+α choose n) has no analogue.

annotations.label: Jacobi orthogonality condition
annotations.note: The Jacobi orthogonality integral with weight (1−x)^α(1+x)^β is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Jacobi differential equation

anchor.snippet: The Jacobi polynomials are solutions to the differential equation

annotations.note: Jacobi ODE is not in Mathlib because Jacobi polynomials themselves are absent.

annotations.label: Gegenbauer as special case of Jacobi
annotations.note: Gegenbauer/ultraspherical polynomials are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Legendre as special case

anchor.snippet: these are called the Legendre polynomials

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Polynomial.shiftedLegendre` (shifted to [0,1]) but no standard Legendre polynomial and no derivation as Jacobi(0,0).

annotations.label: Chebyshev as special case
annotations.note: Mathlib defines Chebyshev T and U directly but does not derive them as a Jacobi specialization.

annotations.label: Hermite polynomials definition

anchor.snippet: The Hermite polynomials are defined by

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Polynomial.hermite` is the probabilists' (He_n) variant, differing by a scaling/sign from the physicists' Hermite used in this section.

annotations.label: Hermite orthogonality
annotations.note: There is no integral-orthogonality lemma for Hermite polynomials against exp(−x²) in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hermite differential equation
annotations.note: The Hermite ODE H_n'' − 2xH_n' + 2nH_n = 0 is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Generalised Laguerre polynomials

anchor.snippet: The generalised Laguerre polynomials are defined by

annotations.note: Laguerre polynomials (generalised or standard) are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Laguerre orthogonality
annotations.note: No Laguerre polynomials in Mathlib, hence no orthogonality result.

annotations.label: Laguerre differential equation
annotations.note: No Laguerre ODE in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Classical orthogonal polynomial differential equation

anchor.snippet: The classical orthogonal polynomials arise from a differential equation of the form

annotations.note: The generic Q(x)f'' + L(x)f' + λf = 0 framework is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Conditions for polynomial solutions

anchor.snippet: There is a series of numbers

annotations.note: The eigenvalue series λ_n giving polynomial solutions to the classical ODE is not formalized.

annotations.label: Properties of the three cases

anchor.snippet: In each of these three cases, we have the following

annotations.note: The structural classification by leading-coefficient cases of Q is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Rodrigues' formula

anchor.snippet: This is known as Rodrigues' formula

annotations.note: Only a Rodrigues-type identity for shifted Legendre is in Mathlib; no general statement for classical orthogonal polynomials.

annotations.label: Formula for λn

anchor.snippet: Under the assumptions of the preceding section, we have

annotations.note: The closed form for the eigenvalue λ_n is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sturm–Liouville form

anchor.section: Second form for the differential equation
anchor.snippet: This is the standard Sturm–Liouville form for the equation

annotations.note: Mathlib has no general Sturm–Liouville framework or this normalised form.

annotations.label: Third form of differential equation

anchor.section: Third form for the differential equation

annotations.note: The reformulation with weighted derivative coefficients is not formalized.

annotations.label: Properties of derivatives P[r]n
annotations.note: Statements about r-th derivatives of generic classical polynomials are absent.

annotations.label: Mixed recurrences for derivatives

anchor.snippet: There are also some mixed recurrences

annotations.note: These cross-derivative recurrences for the generic family are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Orthogonality derivation
annotations.note: The general derivation of orthogonality from the Sturm–Liouville equation is not formalized.

annotations.label: Jacobi-like reduction

anchor.snippet: Every Jacobi-like polynomial sequence can have its domain shifted

annotations.note: Classification/reduction to standard Jacobi form is absent.

annotations.label: Laguerre-like reduction

anchor.snippet: Every Laguerre-like polynomial sequence can have its domain shifted

annotations.note: Reduction to standard Laguerre form is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hermite-like reduction

anchor.snippet: Every Hermite-like polynomial sequence can have its domain shifted

annotations.note: Reduction to standard Hermite form is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Jacobi polynomial parameters

anchor.snippet: still have two parameters to be determined

annotations.note: No Jacobi polynomials in Mathlib; α,β parametrisation is absent.

annotations.label: Jacobi's equation
annotations.note: Jacobi's ODE is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Gegenbauer polynomials definition

anchor.snippet: one obtains the Gegenbauer or ultraspherical polynomials

annotations.note: Gegenbauer/ultraspherical polynomials are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Gegenbauer derivatives relation

anchor.snippet: closely related to the derivatives of

annotations.note: No Gegenbauer polynomials in Mathlib, so this derivative relation is absent.

annotations.label: Legendre's equation
annotations.note: (1−x²)P_n'' − 2xP_n' + n(n+1)P_n = 0 is not proved in Mathlib; only the unrelated shifted Legendre exists.

annotations.label: Legendre second form

anchor.snippet: The second form of the differential equation is

annotations.note: The self-adjoint form ((1−x²)P_n')' + n(n+1)P_n = 0 is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Legendre recurrence relation
annotations.note: Bonnet's three-term recurrence for Legendre is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Legendre Rodrigues' formula
annotations.note: Mathlib has the Rodrigues identity only for the shifted Legendre polynomial (on [0,1]), not the standard P_n on [−1,1].

annotations.label: Associated Legendre polynomials
annotations.note: Associated Legendre polynomials/functions are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Associated Legendre recurrence
annotations.note: No associated Legendre objects exist in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Associated Legendre orthogonality
annotations.note: Associated Legendre orthogonality is absent.

annotations.label: Associated Legendre differential equation
annotations.note: Associated Legendre ODE is not formalised.

annotations.label: Chebyshev's equation
annotations.note: Mathlib proves (1 − X²)T_n'' = X·T_n' − n²·T_n, which is exactly Chebyshev's equation.

annotations.label: Chebyshev recurrence relation
annotations.note: `T_add_two` states T_{n+2} = 2X·T_{n+1} − T_n, the standard Chebyshev recurrence.

annotations.label: Chebyshev Rodrigues' formula
annotations.note: The Rodrigues-style representation of T_n via derivatives of (1−x²)^{n−1/2} is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Chebyshev level property

anchor.snippet: These polynomials have the property that, in the interval of orthogonality

annotations.note: `abs_eval_T_real_le_one` gives |T_n(x)| ≤ 1 on [−1,1], and `isLocalMax_T_real`/`isLocalMin_T_real` formalise the n+1 equioscillation extrema.

annotations.label: Chebyshev polynomials of the second kind

anchor.snippet: There are also Chebyshev polynomials of the second kind

annotations.note: `Polynomial.Chebyshev.U` is the Chebyshev polynomial of the second kind, defined by the standard recurrence.

annotations.label: Associated Laguerre polynomials
annotations.note: Associated/generalised Laguerre polynomials are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Laguerre's equation
annotations.note: Laguerre's ODE is not formalised.

annotations.label: Laguerre second form

anchor.snippet: The second form of the differential equation is

annotations.note: Self-adjoint form of Laguerre's equation is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Laguerre recurrence relation
annotations.note: Three-term recurrence for Laguerre polynomials is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Laguerre Rodrigues' formula
annotations.note: Rodrigues representation of L_n is absent.

annotations.label: Laguerre derivatives relation

anchor.snippet: closely related to the derivatives of

annotations.note: Derivative relation between L_n and L_n^{(α)} is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Laguerre application form

anchor.snippet: Laguerre's equation can be manipulated into a form that is more useful in applications

annotations.note: Alternative application form of Laguerre's equation is absent.

annotations.label: Hermite's equation
annotations.note: H_n'' − 2x H_n' + 2n H_n = 0 is not formalised; Mathlib has hermite polynomials but no ODE.

annotations.label: Hermite second form

anchor.snippet: The second form of the differential equation is

annotations.note: Sturm–Liouville form for Hermite is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hermite third form
annotations.note: Third reformulation of the Hermite ODE is absent.

annotations.label: Hermite recurrence relation
annotations.note: Physicists' Hermite recurrence H_{n+1} = 2x H_n − 2n H_{n−1} is not in Mathlib; only the (x − d/dx) probabilist relation `hermite_succ` is present.

annotations.label: Hermite Rodrigues' formula
annotations.note: Mathlib proves the Rodrigues-type identity for the probabilists' Hermite via Gaussian derivatives, not the physicists' version stated in this section.

annotations.label: First few Hermite polynomials

anchor.snippet: The first few Hermite polynomials are

annotations.note: Only `hermite_zero` and `hermite_one` are spelled out, and only for the probabilist convention.

annotations.label: Associated Hermite functions

anchor.snippet: One can define the associated Hermite functions

annotations.note: Associated Hermite functions (Hermite polynomial times a Gaussian factor) are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Associated Hermite functions orthogonality
annotations.note: L²-orthogonality of associated Hermite functions is not formalised.

annotations.label: Probabilist Hermite polynomials

anchor.snippet: use an alternate definition of the Hermite polynomials

annotations.note: `Polynomial.hermite` is exactly the probabilists' Hermite polynomial He_n, defined recursively by hermite (n+1) = X·hermite n − (hermite n)'.

annotations.label: Sonine–Hahn characterization

anchor.snippet: the classical orthogonal polynomials are the only ones such that their derivatives are also orthogonal polynomials

annotations.note: Sonine–Hahn characterization is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bochner characterization

anchor.snippet: Bochner characterized classical orthogonal polynomials in terms of their recurrence relations

annotations.note: Bochner's theorem on classical orthogonal polynomials is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tricomi characterization
annotations.note: Tricomi's characterization is absent from Mathlib.
